what_it_does: ESM CLI shim that re-executes the sibling index-conversations.js script under the current Node binary, forwarding all command-line arguments, inheriting stdio, and exiting with the child's exit code.
observations: Uses Node ESM import syntax and a shebang
Resolves the sibling script via __dirname derived from import.meta.url
Spawns process.execPath with the .js sibling and process.argv.slice(2)
Wires child stdio to inherit and mirrors the child exit code
standouts: Extensionless entry point delegates to a .js sibling via child_process.spawn
conventions: CLI wrapper pattern separating the public entry point from the implementation file
ESM module with a shebang line
Forward argv and propagate exit code
concerns: No explicit error handling for spawn failures beyond exit propagation
Assumes index-conversations.js exists in the same directory
notes: This is a thin Node ESM launcher that forwards CLI invocation to index-conversations.js and mirrors its I/O and exit status.
questions: Why is the public entry point extensionless while the implementation is index-conversations.js?
Is this file intended to be the bin target in package.json?
apparent_themes: Separation of entry point from implementation
intention_signals: Provide a clean, extensionless CLI command that transparently runs the real implementation file while preserving arguments, streams, and exit status.
contribution: A minimal, reusable launcher pattern that lets a package expose an extensionless CLI entry point while keeping the executable logic in a sibling .js ESM module.
summary_for_embedding: An ESM Node.js CLI wrapper that spawns the sibling index-conversations.js script, forwards arguments, inherits stdio, and exits with the child's status.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an extensionless public CLI entry point that delegates execution to a sibling implementation module.
